lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit BLOCKER: note-to-code MIS-ROUTED live - clicked [[code:...]] chip emits WikilinkActivated ref_kind code and app.rs:6305-6312 sends every non-document ref_kind to open_loom_block, so a code chip opens the symbol_entity_id as a Loom block instead of the code editor at the symbol line; correct machinery exists (dispatch_code_ref_open, CMD_OPEN_CODE_SYMBOL, open_code_symbol) but is called only by tests and take_pending_code_symbol has no shell drain. Code-to-note dead-ends: mounted NoteRefsPanel click stages pending_navigation + CMD_OPEN_DOCUMENT which nothing drains - silent no-op. Fix: ref_kind==code arm routing to open_code_symbol + shell drain + live-shell kittest driving chip click to mounted code pane; run gated live resolve/find-notes tests.

scope.summary:
Goal: implement bidirectional code<->note cross-references. A user should be able to: (A) from a note, click a code symbol reference (written as [[code:path/to/file.rs#MyStruct]]) and jump to that symbol in the code editor; (B) from the code editor, invoke 'Find notes referencing this symbol' and get a panel listing rich documents that mention this symbol. Expected diff: (a) a new CodeRef inline node type for the rich-text editor that renders a clickable code-symbol chip; (b) a cross-reference resolution service in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/interop/cross_ref.rs that calls the existing backend code-nav APIs; (c) a NoteRefsPanel widget added to the code pane that shows notes mentioning the currently focused symbol; (d) integration tests with a real PG backend proving both directions work.

Files to CREATE:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/interop/cross_ref.rs  — defines CodeRef { symbol_key: String, file_path: String, line_start: u32, line_end: u32 }, NoteRef { document_id: String, document_title: String, block_id: String, excerpt: String }. Provides async fn resolve_code_ref(symbol_entity_id: &str) -> Result<CodeRef, CrossRefError> (calls GET /code/symbols/{symbol_entity_id}). Provides async fn find_notes_referencing_symbol(symbol_key: &str, workspace_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<NoteRef>, CrossRefError> (calls GET /knowledge/search with a query derived from the symbol_key, or GET /knowledge/documents filtered by block ref_value containing the symbol_key).
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/note_refs_panel.rs  — egui widget for the code pane sidebar; takes workspace_id + current_symbol_entity_id; loads NoteRef list via find_notes_referencing_symbol; shows each note as a clickable row that fires bus.dispatch_command("open-document", CommandArgs::DocumentId(doc_id)). AccessKit: panel container author_id="note-refs-panel", role=List; each row author_id="note-ref-{doc_id}", role=ListItem, actions=[Press].

Files to EDIT:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/interop/mod.rs  — add `pub mod cross_ref;`
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs  — add `pub mod note_refs_panel;`
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/editor.rs  — add handling for CodeRef inline node: a custom node type 'code_ref' with attrs { symbol_key, file_path, symbol_entity_id }. Rendered as a monospace chip with a small code icon. On click: call bus.dispatch_command("open-code-symbol", CommandArgs::SymbolEntityId(symbol_entity_id)). Register 'open-code-symbol' on the shared command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs, established for MT-031) so the code editor pane handles it by jumping to the symbol.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/editor.rs  — (a) register 'open-code-symbol' command handler: when received, call resolve_code_ref to get file_path + line_start, open the file in the code pane (or switch to existing tab), jump to line. (b) Add NoteRefsPanel to the right sidebar of the code pane; bind to the currently focused/hovered symbol via a watcher that fires when the cursor dwells on a symbol for 800ms (debounce).
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/editor.rs  — add a slash-command '/code-ref' that opens a code symbol search dialog: fuzzy search via lookupCodeSymbols (GET /code/symbols/lookup?q=...), display results, on select insert a CodeRef node at cursor.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. CodeRef node insertion via slash command: when user types '/code-ref' in the rich-text editor, open a floating search widget (egui::Window). Populate from lookupCodeSymbols({ query, workspace_id }) — api.ts line 2158. On selection of a result insert node { type: 'code_ref', attrs: { symbol_key, file_path, symbol_entity_id } } at caret in the document tree.
  2. CodeRef rendering in rich-text: draw a rounded rectangle chip using egui::Frame with a slightly tinted background. Show the symbol_key (short form: last '::' segment). Entire chip is a Button; click fires bus.dispatch_command("open-code-symbol", SymbolEntityId(symbol_entity_id)).
  3. open-code-symbol command handler in code editor: call GET /code/symbols/{symbol_entity_id} (api.ts line 2152 getCodeSymbol) to get symbol.definition { source_id, line_start, line_end }. Open the file source_id in the code pane (if not already open). Scroll to line_start.
  4. find_notes_referencing_symbol: call GET /knowledge/search?q={symbol_key}&workspace_id={ws_id}&surface=rich_docs or, if the backlinks system (MT-032) is live, scan RichDocBacklink records where ref_value contains the symbol_key. The cleanest path is to call getCodeSymbolReferences (api.ts line 2169: GET /code/symbols/{id}/references) to get callers/callees, then cross-reference with known rich-doc block refs. Alternatively, call GET /knowledge/documents (with text search) if the full-text index includes block content. Document which approach is used in a comment.
  5. NoteRefsPanel shows: document title (truncated to 40 chars), a short excerpt centered on the symbol mention, and a right-arrow icon. Clicking fires OpenDocument.
  6. Debounce for NoteRefsPanel: in the code pane update() fn, track last_symbol_entity_id and last_dwell_start (Instant). If cursor is on the same symbol for > 800ms and it differs from currently loaded symbol, fire find_notes_referencing_symbol in a background task.
  7. 'code_ref' node must survive saveRichDocument + loadRichDocument round-trip: the attrs dict must be preserved in JSONContent. Guard: on load, if symbol_entity_id is Some but getCodeSymbol returns 404, render the chip as 'unresolved' (greyed out) without crashing.
  8. Linking back from CodeSymbolPanel.tsx (React reference): the React CodeSymbolPanel already shows definition + file lens. The native version extends this with a 'Notes mentioning this symbol' section — that is the NoteRefsPanel widget's purpose.
  9. AccessKit: CodeRef chip in rich-text uses author_id="code-ref-chip-{symbol_entity_id}", role=Button, actions=[Press]. The symbol search dialog uses author_id="code-symbol-search", role=Dialog; the search input author_id="code-symbol-search-input", role=TextField. Reuse the existing AccessKit registration surface (accessibility/registry.rs, accessibility/live.rs).
  10. Tests: src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_code_note_cross_ref.rs — (a) unit: parse [[code:src/main.rs#MyStruct]] extracts file_path='src/main.rs', symbol='MyStruct'; (b) unit: code_ref node round-trips through JSONContent serialization with all attrs; (c) integration (real PG): call getCodeSymbol for a known test symbol (pre-seeded in test PG), assert definition fields non-empty; (d) integration: create a rich doc with a code_ref node, save, reload, assert code_ref node and symbol_entity_id preserved; (e) kittest: click a code_ref chip, assert open-code-symbol command dispatched with correct symbol_entity_id.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-031 (shared command bus + command dispatch, built on command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) merged.
  - MT-032 (loom block addressing — needed for cross-ref to live in the block backlink system) merged.
  - MT-007 (code editor jump-to-line / file-open infrastructure) merged.
  - MT-012 (rich-text slash commands infrastructure) merged.
  - WP-011 reuse (cross-WP): the shared command bus modules command_registry.rs and event_bus.rs and the AccessKit registration modules under accessibility/ are delivered by WP-KERNEL-011 and are reused directly; the loom graph surface (loom_graph.rs) is also available from WP-011.
  - Backend: GET /code/symbols/{id} and GET /code/symbols/lookup must be reachable — confirmed api.ts lines 2152–2158.

Risk if missed: the code<->note cross-reference edge is explicitly listed in the interconnection contract. Without it, code symbols are isolated from the knowledge surface, the parity proof suite cannot validate this edge, and the 'melt-together' claim is unproven for the code+note pair.
acceptance_criteria: Inserting a code_ref node via '/code-ref' slash command, selecting a symbol from the live backend lookup, saves and reloads the document with the symbol_entity_id intact — proven by integration test against real PG.

Clicking a code_ref chip in the rich-text pane dispatches 'open-code-symbol' with the correct symbol_entity_id, and the code pane scrolls to the correct line — proven by kittest: assert code pane's visible line range contains line_start after dispatch.

NoteRefsPanel in the code pane lists at least one note when a rich doc containing a code_ref to the current symbol exists in the PG — proven by integration test: create rich doc with code_ref, dwell on that symbol in code pane, assert NoteRefsPanel shows the doc title.
An unresolved code_ref (symbol deleted from backend) renders as a greyed-out chip with 'unresolved' label and does not crash or panic — proven by unit test simulating a 404 response.
AccessKit nodes: 'code-ref-chip-{symbol_entity_id}' (Button), 'note-refs-panel' (List), 'code-symbol-search' (Dialog) all present in correct pane context — proven by AccessKit tree dumps.
cargo test -p handshake-native code_note_cross_ref -- --nocapture passes with no failures.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native code_note_cross_ref -- --nocapture  =>  all PASS
integration test (real PG): create rich doc with code_ref node, save, reload, assert symbol_entity_id preserved
kittest: click code_ref chip 'code-ref-chip-{id}', assert 'open-code-symbol' command received by bus
integration test: pre-seed rich doc with code_ref for symbol S; in code pane focus symbol S; assert NoteRefsPanel shows the doc
unit test: getCodeSymbol returns 404 => code_ref chip renders as 'unresolved', no panic
implementation_notes: lookupCodeSymbols maps to GET /code/symbols/lookup?q=... — api.ts line 2158. Params: CodeSymbolLookupQuery { query: String, workspace_id: Option<String>, file_path_filter: Option<String>, limit: Option<u32> }.

getCodeSymbol maps to GET /code/symbols/{symbol_entity_id} — api.ts line 2152. Returns CodeSymbolResponse { symbol: CodeSymbolNavProjection { symbol_key, display_name, kind, staleness, definition: { source_id, line_start, line_end } } }.
getCodeSymbolReferences maps to GET /code/symbols/{id}/references — api.ts line 2169. Returns CodeSymbolReferencesResponse { symbol, callers: CodeSymbolReference[], callees: CodeSymbolReference[] }.

For find_notes_referencing_symbol: the simplest first-pass approach is to search the RichDocBacklink table for ref_value containing the symbol_key (this is what the backend persists when a rich doc saves a code_ref block with ref_value=symbol_key). Call GET /knowledge/backlinks?ref_value={symbol_key} if that filter endpoint exists; otherwise use GET /knowledge/search. Document the chosen path in a code comment.

Reuse the shared command bus from WP-011 (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) for registering and dispatching the 'open-code-symbol' and 'open-document' commands; do not introduce a new bus. Reuse accessibility/registry.rs + accessibility/live.rs for AccessKit node registration.

React reference: CodeSymbolPanel.tsx (api.ts getCodeSymbol, getCodeFileLens) is the parity source for the symbol detail view. The NoteRefsPanel is a new native-only addition (the React version does not have this cross-ref panel, making it a genuine interconnection improvement).

code_ref = hsLink ATOM by refKind, NOT a new 'code_ref' node (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23 - the THIRD instance: MT-014 images, MT-033 atelier, now MT-034 code; RISK-4 confirms it): a code reference inside a note is the EXISTING Child::HsLink atom with ref_kind='code'/'code_symbol' + ref_value=symbol_key (and/or symbol_entity_id in attrs) - so it ROUND-TRIPS the backend content_json (AC-1 save/reload) and the backend backlink indexer picks up ref_value. Do NOT invent a 'code_ref' node type the backend strips on save. The [[code:path#Symbol]] wikilink syntax is parsed by MT-011's wikilink parser -> an hsLink atom whose 'code:' prefix is the refKind discriminator. Reuse MT-016's slash-command infra for '/code-ref' (rich_editor/slash/) + MT-014's hsLink embed renderer for the chip.

open-code-symbol REUSES MT-030's ShellNavigator SEAM (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - the E11 host-mount carry): clicking a code-ref chip dispatches open-code-symbol on the MT-031 bus, which routes via MT-030's ShellNavigator.open_code_symbol(symbol_entity_id). The code editor PANE is NOT mounted in the shell until E11/MT-069 - so MT-034 wires (a) the CodeRef->bus dispatch, (b) resolve_code_ref (GET /code/symbols/{id} -> source_id+line_start), and the actual jump-to-line lands when the code pane is mounted (E11). Until then open-code-symbol stages the target on the ShellNavigator seam (the EditorPaneNotMounted typed status, like MT-030's open_document) - do NOT fake a jump into a non-existent pane. Reuse MT-007's jump-to-line logic for when the pane IS present. Carry-forward already recorded to MT-069.

VERIFY find-notes ENDPOINT + ENCODE KEYS (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - MT-022/029 route lesson + RISK-1/2): find_notes_referencing_symbol must VERIFY the real backend path read-only - since code-refs are hsLink atoms with ref_value=symbol_key, prefer the backlink/ref_value index (the MT-178 backlinks subsystem MT-024 used: GET /loom/blocks/.../backlinks or a ref_value filter) over a naive full-text /knowledge/search (RISK-1: full-text gives false positives on any note mentioning the word). Confirm the exact endpoint + DOCUMENT the chosen path in a comment; if only full-text exists, add a code_ref filter. PERCENT-ENCODE all symbol_key values (they contain :: / #) before embedding in any URL (RISK-2/MC-2) - unit-test a key with '/', '#', '::'; use the percent_encoding crate (verify it is already a dep - reqwest pulls it; else add). getCodeSymbol/lookup/references (api.ts 2152/2158/2169) - verify the shapes.

BACKEND PROOF + HYGIENE (MT-008/030/033 pattern): the [[code:path#Symbol]] parse, the hsLink-code round-trip through DocJson, percent-encoding, the 800ms dwell-debounce (reset timer per cursor move, fire ONCE per threshold crossing - the MT-015/024/030 debounce-cancel pattern, no backend spam, RISK-3/MC-3), and the unresolved-404 chip (greyed, no panic, RISK pt(e)) are unit/kittest-testable STANDALONE. The live getCodeSymbol resolve + find-notes + save/reload round-trip are REAL-PG -> bind real + gate behind integration + NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF. NoteRefsPanel async load (no perpetual spinner, MT-015 lesson). NO Color32 literal (theme guard - the code-ref chip tint reads a palette token like MT-028's). Screenshots EXTERNAL + assert_no_local_artifact_dir(). ACCESSKIT VARIANT: code-ref-chip-{id}(Button)/note-refs-panel(List)/note-ref-{doc}(ListItem)/code-symbol-search(Dialog) - field-correct accesskit 0.21.1. Paths: rich_editor/ not rich_text/ (CodeRef via document_model hsLink + rich_editor_widget render + slash/); code_editor/panel.rs not editor.rs (the MT-007/008 code pane state + jump-to-line).
ports_from_react: app/src/components/CodeSymbolPanel.tsx — getCodeSymbol, getCodeFileLens, definition/staleness display pattern, symbolFilePath helper (reference only; port logic into src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/editor.rs and interop/cross_ref.rs)
app/src/components/CodeSymbolPanel.test.tsx — test patterns for symbol lookup and file lens loading (reference only; port into src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_code_note_cross_ref.rs)
binds_backend_api: GET /code/symbols/{symbol_entity_id}  =>  api.ts line 2152 getCodeSymbol  =>  returns CodeSymbolResponse with definition { source_id, line_start, line_end }
GET /code/symbols/lookup  =>  api.ts line 2158 lookupCodeSymbols  =>  params CodeSymbolLookupQuery  =>  returns CodeSymbolLookupResponse
GET /code/symbols/{id}/references  =>  api.ts line 2169 getCodeSymbolReferences  =>  returns { callers, callees }
GET /code/files/lens  =>  api.ts line 2175 getCodeFileLens  =>  params { relative_path, content_hash, parser_version }  =>  returns CodeFileLensResponse
GET /knowledge/search or GET /knowledge/backlinks?ref_value={symbol_key}  =>  find notes referencing symbol (exact endpoint to be confirmed against backend source)

risks: find_notes_referencing_symbol may not have a direct backend endpoint; using full-text search with the symbol_key as a query string will produce false positives (any note mentioning the word 'MyStruct' regardless of context).
Symbol keys contain colons and slashes (e.g. 'src/main.rs#MyStruct::new') — URL-encoding must be applied correctly in every backend call; missed encoding causes 404s.
Dwell-based NoteRefsPanel load fires on every cursor move if the debounce timer is not reset correctly, spamming the backend with search requests.
code_ref node type not declared in the document schema causes saveRichDocument to strip it silently (if the backend validates node types) or corrupt the document.
open-code-symbol command received by bus but code pane is not open: command handler must open or focus the code pane first before scrolling.
minimum_controls: Document the find_notes_referencing_symbol endpoint choice (backlink filter vs. full-text) before implementing; if full-text is used, add a 'code_ref' filter param to limit to documents containing code_ref blocks only.
Apply percent_encoding to all symbol_key values before embedding in URLs; unit test with a symbol_key containing '/', '#', and '::'.
Use a single Instant + symbol_entity_id pair for dwell tracking; reset the timer on every cursor move; fire the search only once per dwell crossing the 800ms threshold.
Declare 'code_ref' as an explicit allowed node type in the document schema; if the backend rejects it, raise as a typed blocker.
open-code-symbol handler: if code pane tile does not exist, create it (similar to Stage pane in MT-033) before jumping to line.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + MT-014 hsLink embeds + MT-016 slash + MT-030 ShellNavigator seam + MT-007 jump-to-line + MT-178/024 backlinks + MT-022/029 route lesson
corrections: code_ref = hsLink atom by refKind=code (3rd embed instance) - not an invented node; reuse MT-016 slash + MT-014 chip renderer
open-code-symbol via MT-030 ShellNavigator seam (code pane mounts at E11/MT-069); resolve_code_ref + jump-when-mounted, no fake jump
verify find-notes endpoint (prefer ref_value/backlink index over full-text); percent-encode symbol_key (::/#//)
parse/round-trip/encode/dwell-debounce/unresolved-chip standalone; live resolve+find+round-trip NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF; no spinner; no Color32; rich_editor/ + code_editor/panel.rs paths

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_68aabe71 (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: bf9d1775 fix harden per adversarial review
implementation_result.proofs: AC-1..6 PASS (live-PG halves gated NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF); 1064 lib + 9 ckc-cross-ref + regression (interaction_bus 9, ckc_embed 11, accesskit 5, slash 9) + test_theme green; no screenshot (cross-ref logic + chips/panels inside already-verified editors, proven via AccessKit kittest dumps); no tracked artifacts.
implementation_result.steers_all_held: (1) code_ref = hsLink ATOM by refKind=code (3rd embed-lesson application: MT-014 img/MT-033 atelier/MT-034 code) - refValue=ent-... preserved through DocJson round-trip; NO invented node. (2) [[code:...]] prefix registered in MT-011's wikilink parser (ref_kind=code); reused MT-016 slash (/code-ref) + MT-014 chip renderer. (3) open-code-symbol via MT-030's ShellNavigator EditorPaneNotMounted seam (no fake jump; lands at E11/MT-069). (4) find_notes via the VERIFIED loom search-v2 rich-doc-content-type-filtered (not naive full-text, RISK-1). (5) percent_encode_symbol (RISK-2). (6) SymbolDwellTracker 800ms fire-once debounce (RISK-3). (7) unresolved chip greyed via palette token (no Color32 literal; test_theme passes).
implementation_result.waiver_granted: interop/interaction_bus.rs (CMD_OPEN_CODE_SYMBOL) + rich_editor/wikilinks/parser.rs+inline_view.rs + rich_editor_widget.rs + slash_commands/* + code_editor/mod.rs+panel.rs + backend_client.rs + tests in allowed_paths.
